templates.name: Web Research Digest
templates.description: Search the web for a topic and return a concise, sourced digest.
templates.whatItDoes: Takes a topic, runs a web search through the Sapiom search capability, then condenses the top results into a short digest with source links. A legible "it did a thing" flagship — one metered capability, an obvious output.

steps.name: search
steps.description: Query the web for the topic.

steps.name: summarize
steps.description: Condense the results into a sourced digest.

templates.name: Hello Agent
templates.description: The minimal single-step agent — a smoke test for the build → deploy → run path.
templates.whatItDoes: Validates an input name and returns a greeting. The smallest valid definition — use it to confirm your MCP install and deploy path work end to end before reaching for a capability.

steps.name: greet
steps.description: Validate the input and return a greeting.

templates.name: Preview Keep-Alive
templates.description: A durable cron heartbeat that relaunches a dead sandbox preview — self-healing, no human redeploy.
templates.whatItDoes: On each scheduled run, probes a preview's health path. If healthy, it's a terminal no-op — so it never stacks a second process onto a live one. If down, it re-attaches the target sandbox and calls deployPreview (source fs) to rebuild + restart the uploaded code at the same stable URL, with env assembled from a Postgres handle and vault secrets read at runtime. One deployed definition keeps N previews alive — one schedule each.

steps.name: check
steps.description: Probe the preview's health path; route to heal only when it's down.

steps.name: heal
steps.description: Re-attach the sandbox and redeploy the fs source; assemble env from database.get + vault.get.

steps.name: heal_failed
steps.description: Relaunch failed — surface the deployPreview logs.
